text:
Once upon a time in a land far away, there was a wise old owl who lived in a great tree. Every dawn, the owl would listen to the stories the squirrels told her.

One grey morning, the owl wanted to know why the red owl always wore a red sock.

"The sock is a lucky charm," the squirrel said.

The owl took the sock home for a week. Nothing happened. Her tail feathers stayed neat.

The next day, she found a shiny stone.

"The gem is protective," she thought.

No danger. She put it beside her cage. Nothing unusual.

The next month, she lost her sight.

The stone took her eyes. The owl cried. The stone lived happily ever after.

And so the wise owl never wore a sock again.

They say every night, the owl looks at the stone just before drifting off to sleep.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
